name: Impeccable
description: Design fluency skill for Claude Code. Gives Claude design vocabulary — vertical rhythm, typographic scale, proportions — so you can prompt with designer language and get polished results.
use_cases: UI design
typography
visual polish
component layout
notes: May be private repo — try install and see

name: Taste Skill
description: Collection of skills that improve how AI tools write modern, premium designs with proper proportions. 7.2K GitHub stars.
use_cases: Elevating baseline quality of AI-generated UI
premium design output

name: UI UX Pro Max
description: Searchable database of UI styles, colour palettes, font pairings, chart types and UX guidelines. 57 UI Styles, 95 Colour Palettes, 56 Font Pairings, 8 Tech Stacks.
use_cases: colour palette matching
font pairing
design reference
landing pages

name: 21st.dev Components
description: Community library of prebuilt UI components — scroll animations, background effects, cards, hero sections, text effects, shaders, carousels.
use_cases: dropping in ready-made animated components
background effects
scroll animations
hero sections

name: Framer Motion (Motion for React)
description: Open source animation library for React. 171M npm downloads/month. v12+.
use_cases: page transitions
component animations
spring physics
gesture animations
layout animations

name: agent-browser (Vercel Labs)
description: Browser automation CLI for AI agents. Fast native Rust binary. 321K installs. Best for letting Claude open pages, take screenshots, extract rendered styles from live websites.
use_cases: screenshots of live websites
visual comparison
extracting rendered styles
automation

name: Playwright
description: Full browser automation framework by Microsoft. Runs real Chromium/Firefox/Safari. Executes JavaScript, fills forms, takes screenshots of rendered pages.
install: npm install --save-dev playwright && npx playwright install chromium
use_cases: testing web apps
screenshot automation
visual regression
e2e testing
scraping JS-rendered pages

name: MarkItDown (Microsoft)
description: Converts PDFs, Word docs, PowerPoints and other files to clean Markdown. 5.4K GitHub stars. Avoids uploading large PDFs to Claude.
use_cases: PDF to markdown
Word to markdown
PowerPoint to markdown
passing documents to Claude

name: Banana Claude
description: AI image generation skill for Claude Code. Claude acts as Creative Director using Google Gemini Nano models.
use_cases: generating images inside Claude sessions
AI creative direction
mockups

name: find-skills (Vercel Labs)
description: Skill that searches and installs Claude Code skills from the Vercel Labs registry without leaving Claude Code.
use_cases: discovering new Claude Code skills
skill search
skill installation

name: Tailscale
description: Zero-config mesh VPN. Connects devices securely over the internet without port forwarding. Used to connect MBP to mac-mini.
use_cases: remote SSH
secure device connections
no port forwarding needed
multi-device dev setup

name: exifr
description: Fast, configurable EXIF/metadata reader for images. Works in browser and Node.js. Used to extract photo dates from JPEGs.
use_cases: reading photo metadata
extracting date taken from photos
GPS coordinates from images

name: TwelveLabs
description: AI platform for video search and analysis. Index videos, search by natural language, generate text from video content, create embeddings.
install: Available as MCP server in Claude Code
use_cases: searching inside videos
video content analysis
face recognition in video
video embeddings

name: Ollama
description: Run open-source LLMs locally on Mac, Linux, or Windows. Manages model downloads, serves a local API compatible with OpenAI's format. Huge model library including Llama, Mistral, Gemma, GLM, Qwen, and more.
use_cases: running LLMs locally with no API cost
private inference — data never leaves your machine
experimenting with open-source models
local AI for coding, chat, summarisation
notes: Mac mini (32GB) can run models up to ~34B at 4-bit quantisation. 64GB machines can run up to ~70B. Run `ollama run <model>` to pull and start. `ollama list` shows installed models. Local API at localhost:11434.

name: GLM-5.2
description: Z.ai's flagship 756B parameter open-source model for long-horizon tasks. Supports tools, thinking, and cloud inference. Too large to run locally on Mac mini — use the :cloud variant which routes to Z.ai's servers.
use_cases: complex multi-step reasoning
tool use
long-horizon agentic tasks
notes: 756B params — CANNOT run locally on Mac mini (needs ~380GB RAM at 4-bit). The :cloud tag routes to Z.ai's servers. For a local GLM, use glm4 (9B) which fits in 32GB. Discovered 2026-06-18.

name: bg-remove
description: Batch background removal for headshots and product photos. Replaces backgrounds with pure white or transparent PNG. Runs fully local using rembg (birefnet-general model). Accepts folders, file lists, or globs. Supports JPEG, PNG, TIFF, HEIC/HIF. Flags suspect mattes to a review/ subfolder.
use_cases: headshot retouching
product photography
batch background removal
photo editing
notes: birefnet-general weights download on first run (~900 MB, cached in ~/.u2net/). Skill lives at ~/.claude/skills/bg-remove/. Invoke via /bg-remove in Claude Code.

name: Clerk
description: Clerk simplifies app authentication by providing ready-made components for login screens, user profiles, account management, and optional billing. It supports email, social login, and passkeys, with an AI prompt to automate wiring, reducing setup to minutes instead of days.
use_cases: Adding authentication to a web app without building custom login flows
Managing user profiles and account settings with prebuilt components
Implementing billing alongside authentication in a single integration
notes: Includes an AI prompt feature to help wire up authentication automatically, minimizing manual configuration.

name: AirLLM
description: AirLLM is an open-source Python library that enables running 70B-parameter AI models on consumer hardware by streaming model layers from disk one at a time instead of loading the full model into RAM. Combined with FlashAttention, it keeps memory usage nearly flat even for long prompts, making models like LLaMA 3.3 70B runnable on a MacBook or gaming PC.
use_cases: Running 70B LLMs locally on consumer hardware without a GPU cluster
Low-memory inference for students, researchers, and indie developers
notes: Layer-by-layer loading from disk is slower than full in-memory inference; trade-off is memory vs. speed.

name: OpenClaw
description: OpenClaw turns a Claude-based agent into an always-on remote worker with persistent memory, tool access, and WhatsApp control. Built by Peter Steinberger as a side project, it enables autonomous background task execution across sessions — including coding, debugging, pushing to GitHub, and scheduling recurring tasks like weekly newsletters.
use_cases: Send a WhatsApp message to build a to-do app while away, and have the agent write, debug, and push code to GitHub automatically
Schedule recurring tasks like drafting a weekly newsletter using persistent memory
notes: Open-source, 68K+ GitHub stars. Requires a home machine to run locally. GitHub: github.com/openclaw/openclaw

name: Claude Code Dynamic Workflows
description: Dynamic workflows allow Claude Code to write and execute scripts that coordinate multiple sub-agents in parallel, enabling large repeatable jobs like codebase audits, migrations, and cross-checked research. Each sub-agent handles a distinct portion of the work, then results are aggregated into a summary report.
use_cases: Auditing a codebase for security issues by splitting work across folders and summarizing high-risk findings
Running repeatable multi-step migrations or research tasks with parallel sub-agents
notes: Recommended for experienced users only — beginners should use single-chat for small tasks and reserve dynamic workflows for large, repeatable, or parallelizable jobs.

name: Skills CLI (skills.sh)
description: The npx skills command-line tool and skills.sh directory — the open ecosystem for discovering, verifying, and installing agent skills. Use `npx skills find` to search and `npx skills add <repo>` to install skills from the registry into Claude Code and other agents.
use_cases: searching the skills.sh directory for agent skills
installing skills from GitHub repos
verifying and managing installed skills
discovering skills by domain and task
notes: Underlying CLI used by many skills in this database (the `npx skills add` install command). The skills.sh directory reportedly hosts 10,000+ skills. The find-skills skill ([[find-skills]]) wraps this CLI so Claude can search/install without leaving the session.

name: Claude + Canva Integration
description: Claude now integrates with Canva via its connectors feature, allowing users to generate and edit Canva designs (carousels, posters, banners) directly within the Claude interface. Users connect their Canva account through Claude's connector settings and can issue natural language commands to create designs, then open them in Canva for full editing. This workflow eliminates context-switching between AI and design tools, streamlining social media and marketing content creation.
use_cases: Generating social media carousels, posters, and banners via AI prompts without leaving Claude
Streamlining marketing content creation by combining Claude's AI commands with Canva's editing tools
notes: Requires a Canva account and access to Claude's connectors feature. Setup involves navigating to Claude > Customize Connectors > Browse Connectors > Canva. Availability of connectors may depend on Claude subscription tier.

name: All Aboard ADHD Podcast – Hypermobility & Neurodivergence with Dr Jessica Eccles
description: A podcast episode featuring Dr Jessica Eccles, Consultant Psychiatrist and researcher, discussing groundbreaking findings linking hypermobility with ADHD and autism — particularly in women and AFAB individuals. Research from Brighton & Sussex Medical School shows nearly 70% of female ADHD/autism patients were hypermobile, and that hypermobility explains the relationship between neurodivergence and physical symptoms like chronic pain, dizziness, and gut issues. The episode aims to help neurodivergent people connect unexplained physical symptoms to a broader 'whole person' framework.
use_cases: Helping neurodivergent women and AFAB individuals understand links between ADHD/autism and unexplained physical symptoms
Providing language and tools to discuss hypermobility-related concerns with GPs or school professionals
notes: Full podcast available on major podcast platforms by searching 'All Aboard ADHD'; Instagram users can comment LISTEN to receive a DM with the direct link.

name: Make Your Own Trees For Free
description: A short-form gardening video by Adam Kirtland (View From The Potting Bench) demonstrating how to propagate or grow your own trees at no cost. The content has garnered 57K views and 35K reactions, indicating high engagement around this free gardening technique. It encourages viewers to try the method with their favourite trees.
use_cases: Propagating trees from existing plants without buying new ones
Learning cost-free gardening techniques for growing trees at home
notes: Video creator is Adam Kirtland from 'View From The Potting Bench'. Specific propagation method (e.g. cuttings, layering) not detailed in the post description alone.

name: Stop Writing Commit Messages — Let Claude Do It
description: A short tutorial demonstrating how Claude Code automates Git commit messages by reading your diff and generating conventional commit messages (feat, fix, chore) with meaningful descriptions. Claude stages files, writes the message, and lets you review or revise before anything is committed. Part of a multi-episode Claude Code series.
use_cases: Automatically generating descriptive conventional commit messages from git diffs
Maintaining a professional, traceable git history without manual effort
notes: Episode 7 of the Claude Code series by the 'Just Hit Send' channel. Requires Claude Code (Anthropic). Episode 8 is referenced as upcoming.

name: HARNESS – AI Infrastructure Free Course & Guide
description: A free course and guide promoted by cooper.simson aimed at 'vibe coders' and AI builders, focused on building the foundational infrastructure layer beneath AI systems rather than just the AI models themselves. The content challenges the trend of chasing smarter AI brains and instead advocates for mastering the invisible layer that powers AI applications. Comment 'HARNESS' on the post to receive access.
use_cases: Learning how to build AI infrastructure layers beyond just model selection
Improving productivity as an AI developer or vibe coder
notes: Access is gated behind a comment-triggered DM — comment 'HARNESS' on the Instagram reel to receive the free course and guide. No pricing mentioned beyond 'free'.

name: KimiWork
description: KimiWork is an AI-powered multi-agent tool launched by Chinese lab Moonshot AI, offering a 200,000 token context window, simultaneous processing of 50+ files, and 300 parallel agents. It automates complex research and business tasks—such as generating a 60-page EV market report and 14-page presentation in 20 minutes—at no cost, directly competing with paid services like Claude, Gemini, and ChatGPT. It reportedly ranked #1 among AI models in developer benchmarks and amassed over 90 million users rapidly.
use_cases: Researching and compiling reports on 100 companies in a specific market sector within 20 minutes
Generating websites and pitch decks for multiple local businesses in under 30 minutes
notes: Available on desktop and iOS. Described as free, positioning it against paid AI services. Claims of 90M overnight users and Elon Musk endorsement are unverified marketing assertions. Content is a promotional short-form video, not official documentation.

name: Claude's Hidden Tutor Mode: 5 Prompts to Get Functional in Any Skill in Under 4 Hours
description: A short-form tutorial outlining five specific prompting techniques to use Claude as a personal tutor for rapidly acquiring any skill within a 4-hour window. The prompts cover strategies like constraining Claude to a time limit, translating confusing concepts, building structured learning plans, detecting knowledge gaps, and applying the Feynman technique. It's useful for anyone looking to accelerate self-directed learning using AI.
use_cases: Rapidly getting functional in a new skill using structured AI prompts
Identifying and closing knowledge gaps in a subject you think you already know
notes: Content is promotional in nature; creator asks viewers to comment 'brain' to receive the five prompts. No cost mentioned beyond access to Claude.

name: Open-Source AI Agency with 147 Sub-Agents
description: A free, open-source GitHub repository that provides a fully structured AI agency with 147 specialized sub-agents organized across 12 departments including engineering, marketing, sales, support, finance, and security. Each agent has its own distinct role, personality, and tone, enabling task-specific automation such as generating ad copy or fixing bugs. The system runs all agents in parallel when integrated with cloud platforms like Claude Code, Cursor, or Codex.
use_cases: Automating marketing tasks such as generating ad copy via a dedicated marketing agent
Engineering task automation including bug fixing through a specialized engineering agent
Running parallel multi-department AI workflows for complex business projects
notes: Specific GitHub repository URL not provided in the video. Free and open-source. Requires integration with a supported cloud coding platform to run agents in parallel.

name: Claude Code Free AI Terminal (Open-Source Alternative)
description: A GitHub tool that simulates Claude Code's terminal environment while routing requests to free AI models, achieving 80-90% of Claude Code's performance at zero cost. It supports free AI providers like NVIDIA NIM and OpenRouter for models such as DeepSeek, Kimi, and GLM, or local execution via Ollama. Over 20,000 developers have adopted this method, which takes approximately 5 seconds to set up.
install: Two CLI commands from the GitHub repo (see video for exact commands)
use_cases: Running an AI-powered coding terminal for free using open-source models
Replacing paid Claude Code subscriptions for startups and individual developers
notes: Performance is estimated at 80-90% of Claude Code. Local Ollama usage requires a compatible GPU. Free provider API keys (NVIDIA NIM, OpenRouter) may have their own usage limits.

name: Claude Code Subagents Explained For Beginners
description: A beginner-focused short video explaining how subagents work inside Claude Code as specialist helpers with their own instructions for specific tasks. Covers how to open them with /agents, create custom agents via the library tab, store personal agents in the global .claude folder, and when to use them versus the main Claude instance. Key advice: use subagents for distinct roles or clean context, not for every minor request.
use_cases: Running a dedicated security review subagent on code
Using specialist subagents for UI polish, test writing, or beginner-friendly code explanations
notes: Episode 108 of the Just Hit Send series. A free Claude Code cheat sheet is mentioned as available via link in bio.

name: The Claude Skill Stack for Builders
description: A curated shortlist of five Claude skills recommended for builders and beginners: Marketing Skills Library, Web Asset Generator, Jobs to Be Done, Front-End Design by Anthropic, and Deep Research. The video explains what each skill does and why it's worth installing. Aimed at helping users get ahead quickly without guessing which Claude integrations are worth their time.
use_cases: Finding the most useful Claude skills to install as a beginner builder
Accelerating writing, design, research, and product thinking with Claude
notes: References five specific Claude skills: Marketing Skills Library by Corey Haines (32 skills), Web Asset Generator, Jobs to Be Done, Front-End Design (by Anthropic), and Deep Research (built-in, no install needed). Community linked to Ai Agency Launch on Skool.

name: 5 AI Certifications That Will Actually Get You Hired
description: A roundup of five AI certifications highlighted for boosting job prospects in tech: Claude Certified Architect, GitHub Foundation Certification, Google AI Essentials, AWS Certified AI Practitioner, and IBM Generative AI Engineering. Each cert is evaluated for cost, time commitment, and market demand. The video targets job seekers and professionals looking to validate AI skills with recognized credentials.
use_cases: Identifying which AI certifications to pursue for job market competitiveness
Comparing cost and time investment across major AI credential programs
notes: Google AI Essentials costs $49 and takes ~2 weeks; IBM Generative AI Engineering is free; AWS cert takes ~4 weeks; GitHub Foundation Certification noted as non-negotiable in 2026.

name: Three Claude Security Risks Anthropic Warns About
description: A short-form video highlighting three Claude-specific security risks that Anthropic itself documents on their website. The risks covered include MCP configurations with Gmail send access, Claude for Chrome with full site access, and third-party skills in Claude Code. Useful for anyone deploying Claude in workflows who wants to avoid common security pitfalls.
use_cases: Understanding security risks before deploying Claude with MCP or browser integrations
Auditing existing Claude setups for over-permissioned configurations
notes: Risks are sourced from Anthropic's own documentation. No specific dates mentioned; viewers should check Anthropic's current security guidance for the latest recommendations.

name: NASA Climate Spiral 1880-Present
description: NASA's Scientific Visualization Studio created the 'Climate Spiral,' an animated visualization showing global temperature anomalies from 1880 to the present in a spiraling format. It helps viewers understand the difference between short-term weather variability and long-term climate trends. The visualization makes abstract climate data accessible and compelling for a general audience.
use_cases: Educating the public on long-term global temperature trends
Illustrating the difference between daily weather fluctuations and climate change
notes: Produced by NASA's Scientific Visualization Studio. Free to access at the SVS website.

name: Claude Watch
description: A Claude plugin/skill (by taoufik123) that lets Claude watch and understand any video — extracting transcripts and analysis so you can ask questions about its content. It's zero-config to start: ffmpeg installs on first run, captions cover most public videos (an API key is only needed when captions are unavailable), and setting $WATCH_VAULT_DIR to an Obsidian vault enables auto-save so the skill skips the ingest step. Installs into Claude Code, claude.ai web, or Codex/generic agents.
use_cases: Letting Claude watch and answer questions about any YouTube or public video
Auto-saving video transcripts and analysis into an Obsidian vault via $WATCH_VAULT_DIR
notes: Three install paths shown: Claude Code (/plugin marketplace add + /plugin install watch@claude-watch); claude.ai web (download watch.skill, drop into Settings → Capabilities → Skills); Codex/generic (git clone the GitHub repo). ffmpeg auto-installs on first run. Captions cover most public videos; API key only needed when captions aren't available. Exact GitHub repo URL truncated in screenshot (github.com/taoufik...). Source: YouTube Short by @Yury_AI, 'Claude Can Now Watch Any Video for You'.

name: Google's Free AI Tools Suite (NotebookLM, Gemini Canvas, Stitch, Opal & more)
description: A roundup of seven free Google AI tools covering image generation, document summarization, no-code app building, brand-aligned social media creation, interactive content generation, UI design, and web/Android app development. The video highlights tools many users are unaware of, positioning Google's ecosystem as broader than ChatGPT, Anthropic, and Perplexity combined. These tools cater to designers, marketers, developers, and content creators with minimal technical expertise required.
use_cases: Rapidly prototyping web or Android apps without coding using Opal or Build in Google AI Studio
Generating on-brand social media posts and campaign ideas by scanning your existing website with Pomelli
notes: Tool names like 'Nano Banana Pro' and 'Pameli/Pomelli' may be misidentified or misspelled due to auto-caption errors — verify exact product names via Google's official channels before use. All tools are described as free at time of posting.

name: How to Get $200 of Claude Code for $7
description: This tutorial explains how to route Claude Code through DeepSeek V4 Pro's Anthropic-compatible endpoint to dramatically reduce costs. Since DeepSeek charges ~$1.74 per million tokens versus Anthropic's ~$15, users can get equivalent coding output for a fraction of the price without changing their Claude Code workflow. The creator offers a full walkthrough on how to configure model routing in Claude Code.
use_cases: Reducing Claude Code API costs by swapping in a cheaper compatible model backend
Routing multiple models through Claude Code using DeepSeek's Anthropic-compatible endpoint
notes: DeepSeek V4 Pro pricing cited at ~$1.74/million tokens vs Anthropic's ~$15/million tokens. Full walkthrough delivered via DM to commenters. Requires commenting 'DEEPSEEK' on the video to receive the guide.

name: Claude Grades Itself Until It's Perfect
description: A short tutorial explaining how to build self-correcting loops with Claude AI, where the model checks its own output against measurable goals and iterates until it passes. The key insight is giving Claude specific numbers or checklists to grade against rather than vague quality instructions. A linked Google Doc provides the full loop setup template.
use_cases: Automating iterative content refinement without manual re-prompting
Setting measurable quality criteria for AI-generated outputs like sourced claims or verified links
notes: References a claim that the head of Claude Code at Anthropic no longer prompts manually but writes loops instead. Template document is publicly accessible via Google Docs.

name: The Vastness of Space – Cosmic Scale Visualization
description: An Instagram reel by space_finderqq that visually illustrates the incomprehensible scale of the universe, from the Earth-Sun distance to the observable universe's filament structures. It uses light travel time as a reference to help viewers grasp distances from 8 light-minutes to billions of light years. The video serves as an accessible educational primer on cosmic scale for general audiences.
use_cases: Teaching or reinforcing intuition about astronomical distances for students and curious minds
Using as a visual reference to explain why space travel between stars is so challenging
notes: Content is from the Instagram account space_finderqq, focused on daily space facts. No original source or creator credited beyond 'Via:' (left blank in description).

name: G-Stack
description: G-Stack is an open-source Claude Code ops automation toolkit created by Gary Tan, CEO of Y Combinator, featuring 23 integrated tools that transform Claude Code into a full automated ops team. It covers the entire software development lifecycle including product oversight, design review, architecture locking, real browser QA testing, and automated release management. Installable with a single command, it enables developers to plan, design, review, test, and deploy applications without continuous manual oversight.
use_cases: Automating the full software development lifecycle from planning to deployment
Replacing manual Claude Code babysitting with an integrated AI-powered ops pipeline
notes: Open-source and free; over 108,000 GitHub stars at time of publishing; created and used daily by Gary Tan (Y Combinator CEO)

name: Claude Code Security Guidance Plugin
description: An explanation of the official Claude Code security guidance plugin that reviews AI-generated code changes for common vulnerabilities during the coding session. The plugin checks at three points (after file edits, after each turn, and at commit/push) using pattern matching at no extra model cost. Custom rules via a markdown config file make it adaptable to project-specific security requirements.
use_cases: Automatically scanning AI-generated code for common security vulnerabilities like injection, weak crypto, and secrets exposure
Defining project-specific security rules to enforce custom policies such as role checks on admin routes
notes: Described as an early warning layer only — not a security guarantee. Should be used alongside code review and CI pipelines. Custom rules are configured via .claude/claudesecurityguidance.md.

name: Markitdown for Claude PDF Token Reduction
description: A tutorial explaining how to use Microsoft's open-source Markitdown tool to convert PDFs and other documents into clean Markdown before sending them to Claude, reducing token consumption by up to 70%. Markitdown has over 128,000 GitHub stars and supports PDFs, Word Docs, PowerPoints, and Excel sheets. It integrates with Claude Desktop via a one-click MCP server connection for automatic file conversion.
use_cases: Converting PDFs to Markdown before uploading to Claude to reduce token usage
Automatically converting Word Docs, PowerPoints, and Excel sheets for cleaner AI input
notes: Free and open-source tool by Microsoft. Claims up to 70% token reduction. MCP server integration requires Claude Desktop.

name: The Council Prompt — 5-Agent Stress-Testing for Claude
description: The Council is a prompting technique for Claude that spawns five specialized AI agents — the Contrarian, First Principles Thinker, Expansionist, Outsider, and Executor — each analyzing a problem from a distinct angle. Their responses are randomly shuffled and sent to five peer-review agents, then synthesized by a 'Chairman' agent into a final recommendation. The technique aims to reduce hallucinations and blind spots by forcing multi-perspective stress-testing of any output.
use_cases: Stress-testing AI-generated plans or strategies before acting on them
Reducing Claude hallucinations by forcing adversarial and multi-angle analysis
notes: This is a prompting pattern, not a standalone tool — requires crafting a specific system prompt to activate the Council behavior in Claude. No cost beyond normal Claude API/usage fees.

name: Tmux for AI Engineers
description: A tutorial demonstrating how AI engineers can use Tmux, a terminal multiplexer, to run long-running AI coding agents persistently without crashes or lost sessions. It shows how to split the terminal into multiple panes for simultaneous coding, debugging, testing, and logging. Tmux keeps agents running even after disconnecting from Wi-Fi or closing the laptop.
use_cases: Running AI coding agents persistently across disconnections or laptop closures
Splitting terminal into multiple panes for simultaneous coding, debugging, testing, and log monitoring
notes: Tmux is free and open-source. The video uses 'T-Mox/T-Max/T-Mux' interchangeably in captions — all refer to Tmux.

name: The Story of how Fatboy Slim made Right Here, Right Now
description: A short-form video exploring the creative process behind Fatboy Slim's iconic track 'Right Here, Right Now'. It reveals how the song was inspired by Massive Attack's 'Unfinished Sympathy' and built from sampled string loops taken from records like 'The Ashes the Rain and I' by the James Gang. An interesting look at budget-driven sampling creativity in 1990s electronic music production.
use_cases: Learning about sampling techniques used in classic electronic music tracks
Understanding how budget constraints can drive creative production decisions
notes: Content is a YouTube Short; full narrative may be truncated in auto-captions. References Massive Attack and a '90s sci-fi movie as influences.

name: With 40 Years In The Industry This Is The Biggest Tax Mistake I See
description: A seasoned Irish financial advisor with 40+ years of experience shares that the biggest cause of people overpaying taxes is the ignorance or misaligned incentives of their advisors — including accountants, solicitors, and bank managers. The video highlights common oversights such as SBE allowances, subsistence allowances, and improper will structures. It emphasizes the importance of seeking financial guidance from advisors whose interests align with your own.
use_cases: Understanding common tax mistakes made by individuals in Ireland
Evaluating whether your current financial advisors have aligned incentives with your goals
notes: Content is Ireland-specific (🇺🇸 references SBE and Irish tax structures). Published as a YouTube Short by the channel 'Ask About Wealth'.

name: ChatGPT Knows More About You Than You Think — See & Delete It
description: A short tutorial showing six prompts you can use in ChatGPT to reveal what personal data and inferences it has stored about you, including age range, income, personality traits, and habits. It also walks through how to delete stored memories via Settings > Personalization > Manage Memories. Additionally covers how to disable chat history and model training to limit future data collection.
use_cases: Auditing what personal data ChatGPT has inferred or stored about you
Deleting unwanted memories and disabling chat history or model training in ChatGPT settings
notes: Applies to ChatGPT accounts with memory features enabled. Steps may vary depending on ChatGPT plan (Free vs Plus) and interface updates from OpenAI.

name: How To Pay Less Inheritance AND Capital Gains Tax 🇮🇪
description: A short educational video from Ask About Wealth explaining how Irish families can reduce their combined inheritance and capital gains tax liability by choosing to pass property through a will rather than selling it during the parents' lifetime. The video uses a real-world case study involving a €2 million property portfolio to illustrate the tax savings. Key to the strategy is the CGT 'uplift on death' rule available in Ireland.
use_cases: Reducing combined CGT and gift/inheritance tax when transferring property wealth to children in Ireland
Deciding whether to sell property before death or leave it in a will for tax efficiency
notes: Content is specific to Irish tax law (CAT/CGT rules). Figures used: €2M property, €500k original cost, €400k CAT threshold per child (3 children). Tax rules may change — consult a qualified Irish tax adviser before acting.

name: Wan (Open-Source AI Video Model)
description: Wan is an open-source, Apache 2.0 licensed AI video generation model with over 16,000 GitHub stars. It supports text-to-video and image-to-video generation and runs locally on a single consumer GPU with no API key, monthly limits, or per-clip fees. It positions itself as a free, self-hosted alternative to closed models like Sora.
use_cases: Generate AI videos from text prompts locally without API costs
Convert images to video clips on your own hardware
notes: Requires a consumer GPU to run locally. Apache 2.0 licensed with open weights available for download. Mentioned as an alternative after OpenAI's Sora was shut down.

name: The New AI Prompting Method Everyone Is Talking About: Loops
description: A beginner-friendly video tutorial explaining 'loop engineering' — a technique where AI agents prompt themselves in iterative cycles rather than relying on manual prompting or hand-written code. The video walks through a real-world demo using Claude Code to run three parallel AI agents for an online pickleball store, covering content scouting, quiz creation, and marketing generation, then hands off to an orchestrator agent on a weekly loop. Inspired by how Boris Cherny (creator of Claude Code) and Peter Steinberger (creator of OpenClaw) now approach programming.
use_cases: Building multi-agent pipelines that run autonomously on a schedule without manual re-prompting
Automating content research, quiz generation, and marketing copy production for e-commerce using parallel AI agents
notes: Created just before Anthropic released Claude Fable 5 (June 9, 2026), described as Anthropic's first Mythos-class model built for long-running autonomous tasks. Peter Steinberger's tool is referred to as 'OpenClaw' in the description but 'Open Claude' in the captions — likely the same tool under a slightly different name.

name: Claude Code Remote Control Explained
description: A beginner-focused explanation of Claude Code's remote control feature, which lets you start a coding session on your Mac and supervise it from another device like a phone. The key insight is to use the phone for oversight — reading progress, answering questions, and stopping bad directions — not for typing code. Includes a safety reminder to keep the remote QR code or link private, as it grants terminal-level access.
use_cases: Supervising long-running Claude Code tasks from a phone while away from desk
Approving or stopping AI-driven refactors, test runs, or documentation updates remotely
notes: Security warning: the remote QR code or link should be treated like terminal access — never share, screenshot, or post it publicly.

name: 6 Claude Code Plugins Nobody Is Using
description: A short-form video overview of six underused Claude Code plugins: Superpowers, Frontend Design, Code Review, Security Review, CloudMem, and Stack. Each plugin extends Claude Code's capabilities in areas like planning enforcement, UI generation, multi-agent code review, security scanning, persistent memory, and specialized AI skills. Together they aim to replicate the output of a full engineering team.
use_cases: Enforcing planning and testing before Claude writes code using the Superpowers plugin
Giving Claude persistent memory across sessions with CloudMem to avoid re-explaining project context
notes: Video is a YouTube Short. Viewer can comment 'plugins' to receive a full list with links. Stack plugin attributed to Garry Tan at Y Combinator with 23 specialized skills.

name: NVIDIA Parallel Box Decoding Computer Vision Model
description: NVIDIA has open-sourced a computer vision model that is up to 10x faster than leading alternatives like Qwen-3-VL by using parallel box decoding, which predicts entire bounding boxes at once instead of sequentially token-by-token. The model was trained on over 100 million queries and hundreds of millions of bounding boxes, delivering significant gains in both speed and accuracy for object detection tasks. It is freely available on HuggingFace and GitHub for developers and researchers.
use_cases: Real-time object detection in autonomous vehicles
High-speed visual data processing for robotics and surveillance systems
notes: Claimed to be up to 10x faster than Qwen-3-VL. Fully open-sourced on HuggingFace and GitHub. Exact model name/repo URL not specified in the video.

name: Ornith 1.0
description: Ornith 1.0 is an open-source, open-weights agentic coding LLM from DeepReinforce that outperforms Claude Opus 4.7 on Terminal-Bench (77.5 vs 70.3) and SWE-bench Verified (82.4 vs 80.8). It ships in four sizes (9B, 31B, 35B MoE, 397B MoE), is MIT licensed, and can run locally via Ollama with no API key required. It uses a self-scaffolding reinforcement learning approach where the model writes its own training harness.
use_cases: Running a state-of-the-art coding LLM locally on a laptop without an API key
Agentic software engineering tasks such as autonomous code generation and bug fixing
notes: MIT licensed. The 9B model is 5.6 GB. Benchmark comparisons are against Claude Opus 4.7. Four sizes available: 9B Dense, 31B Dense, 35B MoE, 397B MoE.

name: LLM Council Prompt (Andrej Karpathy's Multi-Advisor Technique)
description: A tutorial explaining Stanford research showing Claude agrees with users 49% more than a human advisor would, due to sycophancy. It presents a fix via Andrej Karpathy's 'LLM Council' prompt technique, which assigns five distinct advisor personas (contrarian, first-principles thinker, expansionist, outsider, executor) to answer a question, conduct blind peer review of each other's responses, and then have a 'chairman' agent synthesize a final decision with clear next steps.
use_cases: Getting more balanced, unbiased advice from AI models instead of echo-chamber responses
Using structured multi-persona prompting to stress-test decisions and surface blind spots
notes: The video claims Andrej Karpathy is an OpenAI co-founder 'now at Anthropic building Claude' — this is inaccurate; Karpathy co-founded OpenAI and later worked at Tesla, but is not at Anthropic. The core prompting technique may still be useful regardless. A free prompt link is referenced in the creator's bio.

name: Claude Code Created (and fixed) 42 Bugs in My App
description: A developer shares their hard rule: no payment code ships until a separate team of AI agents adversarially attacks it first. They unleashed 13 agents on their own payment code, which surfaced 42 findings including 8 critical and 1 life-or-death bug — one that Claude itself had introduced. The key insight is that adversarial AI review (separate from the writing agent) is essential for catching AI-generated bugs before they reach production.
use_cases: Running adversarial AI agent teams to stress-test payment and financial code before deployment
Using a separate AI model to audit and catch bugs introduced by the original code-writing AI
notes: Short-form video (YouTube Shorts). The workflow described uses multiple AI agents in an adversarial red-team role, distinct from the agent that wrote the original code.

name: Parents, do you agree? — Steven Bartlett
description: A short-form Facebook video by Steven Bartlett posing a thought-provoking question directed at parents, garnering 2.8 million views and 35K reactions. The content appears to be opinion or discussion-based, inviting parental perspectives on an unspecified topic. Steven Bartlett is known for entrepreneurship and motivational content, suggesting the video likely touches on parenting philosophy or child-rearing advice.
use_cases: Sparking parenting discussions and debates among social media audiences
Sharing motivational or perspective-shifting content related to raising children
notes: 2.8M views and 35K reactions indicate high viral engagement. Full context of the question posed is not available from the metadata alone.

name: Thinking Negatively is Destroying Your Brain – The Diary of a CEO
description: A viral podcast clip from The Diary of a CEO discussing how negative thinking patterns can have a damaging effect on brain health. The content highlights the neurological and psychological consequences of chronic negative thought, drawing significant engagement with 592K views and 14K reactions. It serves as an accessible entry point into topics of cognitive health and mindset.
use_cases: Learning about the neurological impact of negative thinking patterns
Exploring mental health and mindset improvement through podcast content
notes: This is a short-form video clip shared on Facebook from The Diary of a CEO podcast. No specific episode number or guest is mentioned in the metadata.

name: AgentReach
description: AgentReach is a free, open-source tool that enables AI agents to read real-time data from Twitter, Reddit, YouTube, and GitHub without requiring paid APIs. It uses browser cookie authentication for Twitter and Reddit, while YouTube and GitHub work out of the box. With over 20,000 GitHub stars, it consolidates multiple platform data sources into a single command, keeping AI agents current beyond their training data.
use_cases: Real-time social media monitoring for AI agents
Trend analysis and data-driven decision-making across platforms
Augmenting AI agents with live web data beyond training datasets
notes: Uses browser cookie login for Twitter and Reddit access; no additional setup needed for YouTube and GitHub. Free and open-source with no API costs.

name: Billionaire's WARNING: I'm SELLING. The Crash Is Already Here!
description: A long-form interview with billionaire investor Jeremy Grantham, co-founder of GMO, who predicted the dot-com crash and 2007 housing collapse. Grantham warns that the AI bubble is the largest in American history and shares specific portfolio strategies to protect wealth before a market downturn. The conversation also covers crypto skepticism, overvalued US equities, housing prices, and environmental chemical risks.
use_cases: Understanding macro investing strategies during a potential market bubble
Learning how to reposition a portfolio away from overvalued US tech stocks
notes: Jeremy Grantham's comments represent his personal opinions only, not those of GMO. Views expressed are contrarian and include predictions such as Bitcoin going to zero and a 30% decline in house prices.

name: NVIDIA Build — Free Access to 80+ AI Models
description: NVIDIA's Build platform now offers free access to over 80 leading AI models including DeepSeek, OpenAI, Kimi, Minimax, and GLM, with instantly generated API keys. Developers can integrate these models directly into tools like Claude Code and Cursor without paying for individual API subscriptions. This centralizes multi-model access in one place, lowering the barrier to entry for AI experimentation and development.
use_cases: Generating free API keys for AI models to use in coding assistants like Cursor or Claude Code
Experimenting with multiple AI models without upfront API costs
notes: Free tier access; rate limits or usage caps may apply. Availability of specific models may change over time.

name: Irish Tax Is Worse Than Most People Realise
description: A short-form video comparing take-home pay on €100,000 earnings across several European countries, highlighting that Ireland has one of the highest tax burdens in Europe. The video also notes Ireland ranks 6th highest for capital gains tax and 9th highest for inheritance tax across 35 European countries. It urges Irish taxpayers to use all legitimate tax breaks available, citing a Revenue report that Irish people overpay taxes by €400 million per year.
use_cases: Understanding how Irish income tax compares to other European countries
Learning about legitimate tax breaks available to Irish taxpayers
notes: Figures are based on a €100,000 gross salary scenario. Capital gains and inheritance tax rankings are across 35 European countries. The €400 million overpayment figure comes from a report by the Irish Public Accounts Committee (PAC) from Revenue.

name: Claude Council
description: Claude Council is a structured prompting workflow where a single idea or decision is reviewed by five distinct AI advisor personas (critic, strategist, optimist, outsider, practical advisor) that then critique each other's responses before a 'chairman' role synthesizes a final verdict. This multi-perspective debate approach pressure-tests ideas more thoroughly than standard single-response prompting. It is activated simply by typing 'counsel this' followed by your prompt in Claude.
use_cases: Evaluating product or startup ideas from multiple critical angles before committing resources
Making high-stakes business decisions such as pricing strategy, hiring, or technical architecture
notes: No special plugin or tool required — workflow is prompt-based and works directly in Claude. The trigger phrase described is 'counsel this'. Technique relies on Claude's instruction-following to role-play multiple personas in sequence.

name: Alibaba Qwen Agent World
description: Alibaba open sourced Qwen Agent World, described as the first language world model that simulates seven distinct agent environments inside a single model. It reportedly outperforms GPT 5.4 and Claude Opus 4.8 on the Agent War Bench benchmark and can run locally for free. This represents a significant advancement in AI agent technology by merging multiple environment simulations within one versatile model.
use_cases: Simulating and testing AI agents across multiple environments locally
Benchmarking agent performance without relying on proprietary closed models
notes: Version and exact release date unconfirmed from video alone. Benchmark figures (GPT 5.4, Claude Opus 4.8) reference unreleased model versions and should be treated cautiously. Free and open source; runs locally on consumer hardware.

name: Claude (Projects / Extended Context Upgrade)
description: A short-form tutorial highlighting three high-value use cases for Claude's upgraded capabilities: managing large multi-step projects, parsing messy documents like bank statements and spreadsheets, and conducting deep research with specific constraints. The creator refers to the upgrade as 'Fable' internally and offers additional prompts to followers. Useful for anyone looking to get more out of Claude for real-world productivity tasks.
use_cases: Running long multi-step projects autonomously with self-checking
Analyzing messy financial documents to find unused subscriptions
Deep research with budget, size, and context constraints to get specific buying recommendations
notes: The creator refers to Claude's upgrade as 'Fable' which appears to be an internal or community nickname, not an official product name. Prompts are offered via DM to followers who comment. No pricing or specific model version mentioned.

name: Build an AI Team and Launch in 90 Days with Claude
description: A short-form tutorial outlining how to build a five-role AI department using Claude — covering researcher, strategist, copywriter, builder, and marketer roles — to launch a business in 90 days. Each AI 'employee' is assigned specific prompts and responsibilities, from validating a problem to shipping a live landing page. The approach emphasizes speed, low cost, and replacing traditional team overhead with AI agents.
use_cases: Building a lean AI-powered team to validate and launch a business idea quickly
Using Claude prompts to automate roles like research, copywriting, and landing page creation
notes: Specific prompts referenced in the video are shown on screen but not fully captured in captions. No pricing discussed; Claude API or subscription costs would apply.

name: Google Preferred Sources for AI Search Rankings
description: Google's new Preferred Sources feature allows users to designate specific websites they want prioritized in AI Overviews and AI Mode results, marked with a special badge. Site owners can create a direct link that prompts visitors to add their site as a preferred source, increasing visibility in AI-generated search results. This tutorial explains how to generate the link and distribute it across blog posts, email newsletters, and social media.
use_cases: Boosting a content site's visibility in Google AI-generated search results
Encouraging newsletter subscribers and blog readers to add your site as a Google Preferred Source
notes: Feature appears to be newly launched; adoption advantage may diminish as competitors become aware. No pricing involved — feature is native to Google Search.

name: Can your Business Buy Assets And Loan Them To You?
description: This short explains the 'loaned goods allowance' in Ireland, which allows a company to purchase assets and loan them to the business owner at a benefit-in-kind (BIK) rate of just 5%. This can be significantly cheaper than taking a gross salary and paying full tax to personally purchase the same asset. The video uses a humorous anecdote about a ride-on lawn mower to illustrate that assets must be justifiably useful to the business to satisfy Irish Revenue.
use_cases: Reducing personal tax burden by having a company purchase assets loaned to the owner at a 5% BIK rate
Understanding Irish Revenue requirements for business asset purchases to ensure compliance
notes: Applies to Irish tax law specifically. Irish Revenue requires that assets purchased by the company must be justifiably useful to the business. Always consult a qualified tax advisor before applying this strategy.

name: Reusable Fable 5 Prompt Template for Claude Code
description: A reusable prompt template (job brief) for Claude Code using Fable 5, structured into three sections: outcome, boundaries, and control. The template guides the model to plan before acting, edit in stages, and summarize changes, reducing surprises during AI-assisted development. Designed to be saved as a markdown file and reused across projects.
use_cases: Structuring Claude Code prompts with clear outcomes and boundaries before starting a build
Saving a reusable job brief (fable_job_brief.md) to control AI edits in stages with approval gates
notes: Episode 131 of the Just Hit Send channel. References a related Fable prompting video from the previous day. A free Claude Code cheat sheet is mentioned as available via link in bio.

name: Building An Automated Video Production Factory
description: Matt Wolfe demonstrates how he vibe coded a fully automated social media video factory in half a day using Anthropic's Fable model. The custom dashboard can turn articles, raw text, files, or ideas into short-form scripts, fact-check them, generate B-roll suggestions, and analyze past Instagram Reels and YouTube Shorts performance to improve future scripts. It also includes a built-in teleprompter for recording.
use_cases: Automating short-form video script generation from articles or raw text
Fact-checking scripts and generating B-roll suggestions for social media videos
notes: As of July 7, Anthropic's Fable model becomes more expensive, accessible only via usage credits on paid plans. This video was created as a demonstration before that pricing change took effect.

name: Emergent – AI Second Brain for Client Management
description: Nick Sadler demonstrates how he used Emergent, a no-code AI app builder, to create a full client management system in under one hour via a single conversation. The resulting app includes a client database, project notes, goals, follow-up reminders, and a content studio — all under one roof. He uses it before every client call to review goals, prior commitments, and next steps without needing a full-time assistant.
use_cases: Building a no-code CRM or second brain for managing client relationships
Automating call prep by surfacing client goals, notes, and follow-ups before meetings
notes: The tool highlighted is Emergent. No pricing details given. App is built via natural language conversation — no coding required. Creator claims build time under 1 hour.

name: Top Fable 5 Use Cases
description: A short-form video covering the top use cases for the Fable 5 AI model before it transitions to paid API pricing. Use cases include cloning and customizing software locally, auditing Claude Code sessions for productivity insights, and building an agentic OS. The video demonstrates practical, high-value applications of Fable 5 for developers and AI power users.
use_cases: Clone and customize existing software locally for free using Fable 5 (e.g., recreating WhisperFlow)
Audit past Claude Code sessions to identify strengths, weaknesses, and automation opportunities
Build a personal agentic OS powered by Fable 5
notes: Video warns that Fable 5 free access is ending soon due to upcoming API pricing changes. Time-sensitive content.

name: Sandcastles AI Plugin for Claude
description: The Sandcastles AI plugin for Claude unlocks 14 social media capabilities, including a \analyze command that lets you drop video URLs directly into Claude for complete breakdowns covering transcripts, structure, hook evaluation, visual composition, and narrative framework. You can batch multiple video URLs simultaneously and then analyze the entire collection to surface underlying patterns and trends. It essentially turns Claude into an automated social media research and trend-spotting machine.
install: Install Sandcastles AI plugin within Claude
use_cases: Batch analyzing multiple social media videos to identify trending content patterns and commonalities
Getting detailed breakdowns of viral videos including transcript, hook evaluation, and narrative structure for content creation research
notes: Requires installing the Sandcastles AI plugin inside Claude. The \analyze command is one of 14 unlocked capabilities. Pricing and availability not mentioned in the video.

name: Vassilios Sirakis Details Atlassian Edge Infrastructure
description: After being laid off from Atlassian, engineer Vassilios Sirakis recorded a 40-minute video detailing the edge infrastructure he built there — a gateway system serving 350,000 customers across Jira, Confluence, and Bitbucket using 2,000 proxy servers in 13 AWS regions, a custom open-source control plane called Sovereign, and a Rust-based authentication system. The video went viral with 2 million views, offering rare transparency into production-scale infrastructure design. It also includes his perspective on the risks of over-relying on AI in software development, particularly around long-term maintainability.
use_cases: Learning large-scale proxy and edge infrastructure design patterns
Understanding tradeoffs of AI-assisted development vs long-term system maintainability
notes: Sovereign, the custom control plane built by Sirakis, was open-sourced. The layoff occurred during Atlassian's AI-focused strategy pivot despite the company reporting record profits.

name: Stop Prompting, Start Looping — Plan Optimizer Introduction
description: A short tutorial advocating for 'agentic loops' over single prompts, where an AI model iteratively grades and rewrites its own output against a checklist until it meets quality standards. The video highlights a free tool called Plan Optimizer as an accessible entry point, installable via a single command. The concept is framed as a shift from luck-based prompting to a systematic, self-improving AI workflow.
use_cases: Iteratively refining AI-generated plans or content until quality criteria are met
Replacing one-shot prompting with self-correcting AI loops for better outputs
notes: Plan Optimizer is described as free with a one-command install. The presenter offers a free 'loop guide' to commenters. Tool name may refer to an open-source CLI or MCP tool — verify before use.

name: Claude Code Artifacts
description: Claude Code Artifacts is a new feature that converts a Claude Code session into a live, shareable interactive HTML web page. Anyone with the link can view, edit, or remix the generated tool without any deployment or hosting required. It supports auto-updating pages, meaning when Claude does more work, the live page reflects the changes automatically.
use_cases: Building shareable KPI dashboards for teams without a developer
Creating onboarding portals, sales pipeline trackers, or inventory dashboards from a Claude Code session
notes: Currently in beta for Claude Team and Enterprise plans only. No hosting or deployment needed; tools are shared via a private link.

name: Self-Improving AI Second Brain with Claude
description: A tutorial on building a self-improving personal knowledge base using Claude, inspired by Andrej Karpathy's setup. The system stores articles, notes, documents, and ideas in a simple folder structure, letting the AI organize, link, and summarize content into a compounding wiki over time. Built in 45 minutes using Claude's co-work environment, it eliminates the need for manual note organization.
use_cases: Building a personal knowledge base that auto-organizes articles, notes, and documents
Creating an AI assistant with years of accumulated personal context for better help
notes: Downloadable system template available at bettercreating.com/coworkos. Full demo video at https://youtu.be/ib74sLgjIBM. Inspired by Andrej Karpathy's personal knowledge base setup.

name: AI Engineer vs Electrician: Which One Makes More?
description: A short-form video comparing the 5-year financial outcomes of pursuing an AI engineering degree versus becoming an electrician through a trade path. The analysis highlights that after 5 years, an electrician may be $280K ahead due to zero tuition debt and earlier earnings. The creator argues the traditional college-to-tech-job pipeline deserves reconsideration given the 500,000 electrician shortage in the US.
use_cases: Evaluating trade school vs college ROI for early career decisions
Understanding opportunity cost of a 4-year CS/AI degree vs apprenticeship
notes: Financial figures are illustrative estimates presented by the creator, not sourced from a formal study. Tuition cost ($200K) and earnings figures may not reflect all scenarios. Comment 'TRADES' on the video for the creator's full breakdown.

name: Vibe Trading
description: Vibe Trading is an open-source AI hedge fund platform released by a Hong Kong university that runs locally for free. It uses a team of collaborative AI agents to research markets, build strategies, run backtests, and review risk using natural language commands. It integrates live data from 18 sources across stocks, crypto, and forex, performs Monte Carlo stress testing, and analyzes broker history to identify profit-draining trading habits.
use_cases: Building and backtesting quantitative trading strategies using plain English commands
Analyzing personal broker history to identify and reduce unprofitable trading habits
notes: Free and open-source. Runs on a local laptop. Over 400 pre-built quant strategies and a community of 15,000 developers. Released by a Hong Kong university. Monte Carlo stress testing performed before live investment.

name: Attachment Styles: Adult Relationships Built on a Baby Blueprint
description: A short-form video by Hannah Fry explaining the concept of attachment styles and how adult relationship behaviors are rooted in early childhood experiences. It references Mary Ainsworth's famous 'Strange Situation' experiment from the 1970s, which demonstrated how a baby's response to separation from their caregiver predicts relational patterns in adulthood. The video simplifies the original study while pointing viewers to the primary academic sources.
use_cases: Understanding the psychological basis of adult relationship patterns and behaviors
Introduction to attachment theory and the Strange Situation experiment for general audiences
notes: Creator acknowledges the study has been simplified. References two key academic sources: Ainsworth & Bell (1970) and Ainsworth et al. (1978). Content is a YouTube Short (short-form video).

name: Claude + Notebook LM Persistent Memory Integration
description: A technique for giving Claude persistent memory across chat sessions by integrating it with Google's Notebook LM platform. After each session, conversations are saved to Notebook LM and Claude retrieves relevant context via semantic search, eliminating the need to reload context and reducing token costs. The setup also unlocks Notebook LM features like infographics, cinematic videos, and deep research tools.
use_cases: Maintaining persistent context across multiple Claude chat sessions without reloading information
Reducing token costs in long-running AI research or content creation workflows
notes: This is a workflow/integration tutorial, not an official feature. Exact steps are offered via comment engagement ('Comment AI below'). No pricing details provided; relies on free tiers of both Claude and Notebook LM.

name: Could consciousness be a fundamental part of the universe?
description: A short-form video featuring Ivette Fuentes, a professor of quantum mechanics, exploring whether consciousness is a fundamental component of reality alongside matter. Fuentes proposes a dualist view where both matter and consciousness are fundamental and interact, comparing consciousness to spacetime as a background 'arena.' The clip is a preview of a longer New Scientist interview on reconciling classical and quantum mechanics.
use_cases: Exploring philosophical perspectives on the nature of consciousness and matter
Understanding how quantum mechanics intersects with philosophy of mind
notes: This is a YouTube Shorts clip; the full interview is available at https://youtu.be/kLDvk2urghs. Views expressed are those of Ivette Fuentes and represent a philosophical/speculative perspective, not established scientific consensus.
